role: 1st President of the Islamic Republic of Iran (1980–1981)
bio: Abolhassan Banisadr was the first president of the Islamic Republic, elected in January 1980 with 76% of the vote. An economist and political thinker who had been close to [Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) in exile, he had previously served as a member of the [Assembly of Experts for the Constitution](/orgs/assembly-of-experts-for-constitution) in 1979, where he was the second-highest vote-getter in Tehran Province. He quickly came into conflict with the clerical establishment and the [Islamic Republican Party](/orgs/islamic-republican-party). He was impeached by parliament in June 1981 and fled to France, where he lived in exile until his death in 2021.
editorial_notes: Died 9 October 2021 in Paris. Impeached June 22, 1981.
